description: Archetypal creature categories for Leonoria. Each category contains 10 archetypes covering the full threat spectrum from weak to legendary. Specific named creatures are in biomebestiary.json and legendarybestiary.json. Archetypes use Leonoria damage types and aptitude-based modifiers.
difficulty_note: Monsters do not level with characters. Threat is fixed. More dangerous creatures are naturally found further from starting positions on world maps. Perception skill helps teams detect danger level before engagement.
modifier_note: Modifiers shown as decimal multipliers (0.10 = +10% bonus to that stat or damage type).

categories.description: Natural biological creatures of Leonoria. No magical origin. Resisted by physical damage types. Vulnerable to Blight and Venomous effects. Purelight has no special interaction.

archetypes.name: Vermin-Kin
archetypes.description: Weak, prolific scavengers. Rats, insects, bats. Dangerous only in swarms.

archetypes.name: Avian-Strigid
archetypes.description: High-agility flying predators. Owls, hawks, corvids of unusual size.

archetypes.name: Cunning-Canid
archetypes.description: Pack hunters with tracking instincts. Wolves, hyenas, wild dogs.

archetypes.name: Great-Feline
archetypes.description: Stealth ambush predators. Large cats, panthers, direwolves.

archetypes.name: Heavy-Ursine
archetypes.description: Massive beasts with high physical staying power. Bears, boars, rhino-like creatures.

archetypes.name: Plated-Reptilian
archetypes.description: Natural armor plating against physical strikes. Crocodilians, giant lizards.

archetypes.name: Tusked-Pachyderm
archetypes.description: Unstoppable force. Ignores light obstacles and terrain. Charges deal Rupturing secondary.

archetypes.name: Aquatic-Apex
archetypes.description: Crushing hydraulic pressure from the deep. Massive aquatic predators.

archetypes.name: Dune-Colossus
archetypes.description: Living mountains found in shifting sands and Badlands crags. Near-immune to physical.

archetypes.name: Apex-Chimera
archetypes.description: A biological masterpiece of multiple lethal traits. Unique. Rare. Always dangerous.

categories.description: Reanimated or spiritually bound dead. Found wherever great death and dark magic intersect — primarily The Dark Forests, The Badlands fringe, and The Forgotten Kingdom. Immune to Venomous and Poisonous. Purelight deals bonus damage to all undead.

archetypes.name: Shambling-Husk
archetypes.description: Barely animated corpse. Slow. Dangerous only in numbers or enclosed spaces.

archetypes.name: Brittle-Frame
archetypes.description: Animated skeleton. Resists Slashing (no flesh). Very vulnerable to Blunt.

archetypes.name: Stitched-Brute
archetypes.description: Multiple corpses stitched into one huge body. Enormous HP. Slow.

archetypes.name: Bone-Sentinel
archetypes.description: Armed skeleton warrior maintaining some martial memory from life.

archetypes.name: Blight-Carrier
archetypes.description: A walking infection. Every hit spreads Blight status. Can contaminate an area.

archetypes.name: Ossified-Knight
archetypes.description: Armored skeletal warrior. Retains tactical combat awareness. High physical resistance.

archetypes.name: Grave-Horror
archetypes.description: A blood-drinking undead predator. Targets living blood. Spreads Darkblood status.

archetypes.name: Lich-Puppet
archetypes.description: A magically reanimated skeleton with retained spell-casting ability. Controlled by a Lich or Voidweaver.

archetypes.name: Blood-Starved Hulk
archetypes.description: An undead abomination that heals itself from blood of living targets. Extremely dangerous.

archetypes.name: Death-Lord Vessel
archetypes.description: A Lich or death-lord inhabiting a constructed vessel body. Near-invulnerable to conviction attacks. Legendary threat.

categories.description: Entities from beyond the normal fabric of Leonoria — connected to the void, the shadow realm, or eldritch presences that predated The Great Death or were born from it. All Abyssal creatures are at least partially immune to physical damage. Purelight is generally effective.

archetypes.name: Void-Skitterer
archetypes.description: A small void-touched creature that skitters across surfaces including walls and ceilings.

archetypes.name: Shadow-Stalker
archetypes.description: A shadow that moves. Invisible in darkness. Hunts by Conviction level — targets the most afraid.

archetypes.name: Entropy-Leech
archetypes.description: Feeds on body heat and life energy. Physical contact drains Metabolism rapidly. Cannot be seen clearly — blurs vision.

archetypes.name: Dark-Judicator
archetypes.description: A demonic enforcer from the shadow realm. Intelligent, purposeful. Arrives with specific intent and does not leave until it is achieved or it is destroyed.

archetypes.name: Eldritch-Stargazer
archetypes.description: An entity that exists partially outside normal reality. Looking directly at it requires a Conviction check. Attacks reality as much as it attacks bodies.

archetypes.name: Void-Knight
archetypes.description: An armored demonic warrior made of condensed void energy. Bleeds darkness. Nearly immune to physical damage.

archetypes.name: Gazing-Horror
archetypes.description: A floating, eye-covered entity that targets Conviction directly. Looking at it causes Tenacity drain. Killing it requires destroying each eye.

archetypes.name: Void-Engine
archetypes.description: A massive void creature that generates atmospheric chaos. Storms follow it. Its presence disrupts all ranged magical attacks.

archetypes.name: Eater of Suns
archetypes.description: An enormous abyssal beast that consumes light. Purelight attacks are significantly reduced. Burns with dark fire. Ancient and terrible.

archetypes.name: Void-Architect
archetypes.description: An entity that rewrites local reality. Encounters with it age heroes (Temporal damage per turn in its presence). Legendary. May be immortal.

categories.description: Animate plant and fungal creatures. Immune to Venomous and Poisonous. Weak to Fire and Slashing. Found primarily in Sanctuary Lands, Boglands, and Dark Forests. Purelight has no special interaction.

archetypes.name: Creeping-Vine
archetypes.description: Animated vines that grab and cut. Found at the edge of forests.

archetypes.name: Fungal-Spore
archetypes.description: A spore-releasing fungal cluster. Clouds of spores cause Blight in a radius.

archetypes.name: Athropic-Shrub
archetypes.description: A life-draining bush that saps the energy from nearby living things through root contact.

archetypes.name: Thorny-Sentinel
archetypes.description: A thorned mobile plant that defends territory around water sources.

archetypes.name: Iron-Bark Treant
archetypes.description: A massive ancient tree that has become aware. Very high physical resistance. Slow but devastating.

archetypes.name: Living-Marsh
archetypes.description: A section of bog that has become a single creature. Engulfs targets that step into it.

archetypes.name: Sentinel-Oak
archetypes.description: A guardian oak with martial awareness. Commands other plants in its radius.

archetypes.name: Venom-Bloom
archetypes.description: A beautiful flower that is extremely dangerous. Area Blight pulses. Touch is lethal. Beloved by Artisans for its extreme alchemical value.

archetypes.name: World-Root
archetypes.description: A root system that has achieved consciousness over millennia. Controls the terrain around it. Temporal attacks age the ground itself.

archetypes.name: God-Tree Husk
archetypes.description: The animated corpse of a world-tree that died in The Great Death. Ancient beyond measure. Its Temporal attacks permanently age heroes caught in its radius.

categories.description: Entities of light, faith, and the upper Materium spectrum. Rare — almost never hostile unless provoked or bound by dark magic. Dark-aligned characters may find them hostile by nature. Shadow Arts deal reduced damage.
categories.purelight_interaction: immune (cannot be damaged by own school)

archetypes.name: Light-Spark
archetypes.description: A small mote of living light. Harmless unless threatened.

archetypes.name: Solar-Wisp
archetypes.description: A fire-and-light elemental. Warm to nearby characters. Hostile when dark magic is used nearby.

archetypes.name: Aegis-Shell
archetypes.description: A reflective light-construct. Reflects a portion of incoming damage back as Aegis Reflection.

archetypes.name: Truth-Blade
archetypes.description: A manifestation of divine judgment. Deals catastrophic Verdicium damage scaled to a target's guilt (Scarring stat).

archetypes.name: Materium-Shield
archetypes.description: A guardian construct of blessed materium. Protects sacred locations.

archetypes.name: Solar-Seraph
archetypes.description: A winged light-warrior of significant power. Actively hunts undead and dark arts users.

archetypes.name: Celestial-Scribe
archetypes.description: A knowledge-entity. Non-violent by default. Attacks by forcibly calming and revealing the target's guilt.

archetypes.name: Core-Guardian
archetypes.description: An ancient guardian of a sacred location. Near-immortal within its territory. Cannot be permanently killed there — only driven back.

archetypes.name: High-Justiciar
archetypes.description: A towering entity of divine judgment. Its presence causes Verdicium damage passively to all dark-aligned characters in range.

archetypes.name: Materium-Prime
archetypes.description: A manifestation of pure Light Materium. Legendary. Rarely seen. Some cultures worship it as a deity. Its very presence heals light-aligned characters.

categories.description: Partially immaterial beings tied to specific Materium energies or emotional states. Immune to mundane Physical damage (Blunt/Slashing/Piercing). Must be hit with magical damage. Originate from locations where specific energies are concentrated.
vulnerabilities: Damage type matching their origin school (+20%)

archetypes.name: Mist-Echo
archetypes.description: A wind-spirit that exists as moving fog. Very hard to hit. Deals gentle Tempest damage.

archetypes.name: Frost-Specter
archetypes.description: An undead-adjacent specter suffused with supernatural cold. Drains Metabolism rapidly.

archetypes.name: Static-Phantom
archetypes.description: A storm-energy phantom with high initiative. Shock cascade triggers on every hit.

archetypes.name: Blight-Vapor
archetypes.description: A cloud of animate Blight energy. Area effect Blight aura. Cannot be physically outrun.

archetypes.name: Arcane-Flare
archetypes.description: A volatile Arcane energy construct. Especially dangerous to unarmored magic users — its own school's rules apply.

archetypes.name: Void-Ghost
archetypes.description: A ghost born from a Darkvoid event. Highly unstable — the damage variance of Darkvoid applies to this creature's attacks unpredictably.

archetypes.name: Temporal-Blur
archetypes.description: An entity moving out of sync with normal time. Attacks age targets. Cannot be reliably hit without Perception above 40.

archetypes.name: Solar-Phantom
archetypes.description: A fragment of a dying celestial entity. Only attacks dark-aligned characters. Ignores all others.

archetypes.name: Soul-Fragment
archetypes.description: A fragment of a person's soul that refused to pass on. Attacks by forcibly removing the will to fight. Does not kill directly — just removes initiative.

archetypes.name: Void-Wraith
archetypes.description: A powerful void-wraith that embodies combined Shadow school energies. Saps Conviction and Metabolism simultaneously. Partially resistant to its own school.

categories.description: Pure manifestations of Materium elemental schools. Immune to their own element. Weak to opposing elements. Found where Materium energy concentrates naturally — volcanic zones, deep sea, high peaks, storm regions.
vulnerabilities: Opposing element (+25%): Fire↔Aquas, Earthen↔Tempest, Impact (universal weak point)

archetypes.name: Dust-Skitterer
archetypes.description: A small earth-elemental. Barely dangerous alone; terrain-bonded.

archetypes.name: Cinder-Mite
archetypes.description: A flame-insect elemental. Sets surroundings on fire. Very fast.

archetypes.name: Vapor-Sprite
archetypes.description: A water elemental sprite. Small but able to Soak targets, triggering Tempest synergy.

archetypes.name: Zephyr-Wisp
archetypes.description: A wind-elemental with the highest initiative of any small creature. Cascade triggers on every hit.

archetypes.name: Mud-Lurker
archetypes.description: A hybrid earth-water elemental. Immobilizes targets in deep mud. Found in bogs.

archetypes.name: Flame-Revenant
archetypes.description: A fire elemental that has survived long enough to develop aggression. Ignites terrain on contact. Spreads.

archetypes.name: Storm-Walker
archetypes.description: A large tempest elemental that brings a localized storm with it. Cascade triggers at 25% per hit. Moves with the wind.

archetypes.name: Glacial-Monolith
archetypes.description: A slowly-moving earth-and-ice elemental. Extremely high physical resistance. Crumbles terrain as it moves.

archetypes.name: Magma-Behemoth
archetypes.description: A colossal fire-earth elemental from deep geological activity. Walks through stone. Fire weapon attacks heal it.

archetypes.name: Materium-Titan
archetypes.description: A titan-class elemental that commands all four physical elements simultaneously. Legendary. Possibly a remnant from before The Great Death.

categories.description: Leonoria's most feared natural predators. Ancient, elemental in nature, and almost always dangerous. They do not lair in predictable places — they claim territory. Each dragon type has an elemental affinity that determines both its attack type and its primary resistance. Always a significant threat.
categories.purelight_interaction: neutral to light dragons; dark dragons vulnerable

archetypes.name: Drake-Scavenger
archetypes.description: A young or degenerate drake. Still dangerous. Fast and aggressive.

archetypes.name: Wyvern-Hunter
archetypes.description: A two-legged flying predator with a venomous tail stinger. Not a true dragon but close.

archetypes.name: Cliff-Cutter
archetypes.description: A mountain-dwelling dragon with stone-grey scales. Cuts through rock. Fast in mountainous terrain.

archetypes.name: Sulfur-Spitter
archetypes.description: A fire-breathing dragon with acidic saliva. Classic predator. Commands volcanic or desert territory.

archetypes.name: Frost-Brood
archetypes.description: An ice-attuned dragon. Exhales supernatural cold (Chill, not Climate-Cold). Commands frozen territory.

archetypes.name: Blight-Wing
archetypes.description: A diseased dragon suffused with Blight energy. Its territory is barren. Everything near it rots.

archetypes.name: Storm-Tyrant
archetypes.description: A massive storm dragon that perpetually flies in a self-generated storm. Cascade triggers on every attack. Lightning follows it.

archetypes.name: Abyssal-Wyrm
archetypes.description: A dragon that has been corrupted by or born from Shadow Arts energy. Actively hostile to light-aligned characters. Attacks erode Conviction.

archetypes.name: Celestial-Drake
archetypes.description: An ancient dragon aligned with Lightwielding. Only attacks dark-aligned characters or those who attack it first. Considered sacred by several cultures.

archetypes.name: Timeless-Ancient
archetypes.description: A dragon so old it has partially transcended time. Its breath weapon ages targets permanently (Temporal damage). Resistant to all schools. May be the same dragon across multiple historical eras.
